title: Litter Pick / River Cleanup
summary: Organise a one-day community cleanup of a park, street, riverbank or trail.

title: Repair Café
summary: Host a pop-up event where volunteer fixers help neighbours repair electronics, clothing, bikes and furniture.

title: Clothing Swap
summary: Organise an event where people exchange good-quality clothes instead of buying new.

title: Seed & Plant Swap
summary: Host a seasonal exchange of seeds, seedlings and cuttings for local gardeners.

title: Beach / Lakeside Cleanup
summary: Run a shoreline cleanup with waste sorting and optional citizen-science litter data collection.

title: Community Fruit Harvesting (Fruit Rescue)
summary: Pick unharvested fruit from private and public trees and distribute it to pickers, owners and food charities.

title: Food Waste Cooking Event (Disco Soup)
summary: Cook and share a public meal made from rescued surplus food, with music and conversation about food waste.

title: Book / Toy Swap
summary: A family-friendly exchange event for books, games and toys.

title: E-Waste Collection Drive
summary: A one-day neighbourhood collection of old electronics, cables and batteries for certified recycling.

title: Nature Walk / BioBlitz Citizen Science Day
summary: A guided species-recording event where participants document local biodiversity for real scientific datasets.

title: Birdhouse & Insect Hotel Building Workshop
summary: A hands-on session where participants build nesting boxes and insect hotels for local gardens and parks.

title: Give-Box / Public Bookcase Installation
summary: Build and install a weatherproof public exchange cabinet for books or household items.

title: Wildflower / Pollinator Planting Day
summary: Convert a verge, lawn strip or park corner into a wildflower patch for pollinators.

title: Documentary Screening + Discussion Night
summary: Screen a climate or sustainability documentary followed by a facilitated local-action discussion.

title: Visible Mending / Clothing Repair Circle
summary: A recurring social meetup where people learn to repair and visibly mend their own clothes.

title: Community Garden
summary: Establish a shared garden where neighbours grow food together on unused land.

title: Community Compost Scheme
summary: Set up a shared composting site where households drop off organic waste and receive finished compost.

title: Tool Library / Library of Things
summary: Create a lending library for tools and household equipment so neighbours borrow instead of buy.

title: Community Fridge / Food-Sharing Point
summary: Install a public fridge where surplus food is shared freely between neighbours and businesses.

title: Surplus Food Rescue & Redistribution
summary: Build a recurring collection route that moves surplus food from businesses to charities and community fridges.

title: Repair Skills Training Series
summary: A multi-week course teaching practical repair skills (electronics, textiles, bikes) to build local fixer capacity.

title: School Garden Program
summary: Partner with a school to build and run a teaching garden integrated into lessons.

title: Neighbourhood Tree Planting
summary: Organise the planting of street or park trees with the municipality and residents.

title: Zero-Waste Bulk-Buying Group
summary: A neighbourhood group that orders staple foods in bulk to cut packaging and cost.

title: Community Carpool / Lift-Sharing Network
summary: Set up an organised lift-sharing scheme for a village, workplace cluster or club.

title: Walking School Bus
summary: Organise supervised walking routes where children walk to school together instead of being driven.

title: Textile Upcycling Workshop Series
summary: Workshops transforming damaged or unwanted textiles into new garments and useful items.

title: Plant-Based Cooking Club
summary: A recurring social cooking group exploring affordable, delicious low-carbon meals.

title: Local Green Map / Repair & Reuse Directory
summary: Research and publish a map of local repair shops, second-hand stores, refill stations and sharing services.

title: Play Street / Car-Free Day
summary: Temporarily close a residential street to traffic for a day of play, food and neighbourly connection.

title: Draft-Proofing & Energy-Saving Brigade
summary: Trained volunteers help neighbours install simple energy-saving measures like draft excluders and radiator foils.

title: Hard-to-Recycle Collection Point Network
summary: A network of drop-off boxes in shops and cafés for items kerbside recycling doesn't take (corks, pens, blister packs).

title: Battery & Lightbulb Recycling Awareness Campaign
summary: A local campaign making sure households know where and how to recycle batteries and bulbs.

title: Guerrilla Gardening / Seed Bombing
summary: Beautify neglected urban spots with low-maintenance native planting.

title: Second-Hand Market
summary: Organise a neighbourhood flea market focused on reuse.

title: Climate-Themed Pub Quiz / Games Night
summary: A fun social quiz night that builds community around climate topics.

title: Local Sustainability Newsletter / Blog
summary: A regular publication covering local green initiatives, events and how-tos.

title: Neighbourhood Swap Board
summary: A physical or digital board where neighbours offer and request items and favours.

title: Solar Cooperative / Community Solar Purchase Group
summary: Organise collective purchasing or cooperative ownership of solar installations for local roofs.

title: Community-Owned Renewable on Public Buildings
summary: Develop a citizen-financed solar installation on a school, gym or civic roof, with the municipality as host.

title: Home Energy Audit Volunteer Service
summary: Trained volunteers provide free walk-through energy assessments with prioritised recommendations for households.

title: Rain Garden / Depaving Project
summary: Remove unnecessary paving and create a rain garden that absorbs stormwater and supports biodiversity.

title: Green Roof / Facade Pilot on a Community Building
summary: Install a demonstration green roof or climbing facade on a community-owned building.

title: Citizen Air Quality Monitoring Network
summary: Deploy low-cost sensors across the neighbourhood and publish open air-quality data.

title: Stream / River Restoration Project
summary: Work with authorities to restore a degraded stream section: banks, vegetation and habitat.

title: Hedgerow / Wildlife Corridor Planting
summary: Connect fragmented habitats by planting native hedgerows across cooperating properties.

title: Community Orchard
summary: Plant and steward a shared orchard of local fruit varieties on public or leased land.

title: SME Sustainability Audit Service
summary: Skilled volunteers help small local businesses assess and cut their environmental footprint.

title: Cargo Bike Sharing Scheme
summary: Provide shared cargo bikes so households can move kids and goods without a car.

title: Local Climate Advocacy Group / Citizens' Assembly Push
summary: Organise residents to advocate for concrete municipal climate policies or a local citizens' assembly.
